provenance.citation: Medium composition from PMC6255981 (see GrowthDB record).
provenance.notes: Full recipe recovered from the primary literature: base medium 'Shewanella oneidensis MR-1 defined medium (a.k.a. 'MR-1 medium'), lactate as electron donor, PIPES-buffered; modified here to 20 mM sodium lactate' (Cited in paper as 'modified MR-1 medium (Cao et al., 2011)'. Two candidate Cao et al. 2011 papers on Shewanella sp. HRCR-1 biofilms from the Beyenal/Fredrickson group: (1) Cao B, et al. 2011, Environ Microbiol 13:1018-1031, DOI 10.1111/j.1462-2920.2010.02407.x, PMID 21251176; (2) Cao B,
provenance.verification_note: PMC6255981 does NOT reproduce the modified MR-1 medium formulation (salts, buffer, N source, vitamins, trace elements) - it defers entirely to Cao et al. 2011. Only two facts are verifiable from THIS paper: carbon source = 20 mM sodium lactate ([3-13C] for MFA), and the experiments were run aerobically (shaking flasks 200 rpm; O2-permeable biofilm reactors). S. oneidensis MR-1 is itself facultativ
provenance.recipe_evidence: PMC6255981 (Frontiers Microbiol 2018) Methods: 'S. oneidensis MR-1 and its high c-di-GMP transconjugant for enhanced biofilm formation, as additional tests, were grown in a modified MR-1 medium (Cao et al., 2011) using 20 mM sodium lactate.' Canonical composition of the referenced S. oneidensis defined medium (repeatedly reported for this organism/group): 'a defined medium containing 18 mM lactate as the sole carbon source, 50 mM PIPES, 7.5 mM NaOH, 28 mM NH4Cl, 1.3 mM KCl, 4.3 mM NaH2PO4·H2O, 100 mM NaCl, and 10 ml/liter each of vitamin solution, amino acid solution, and trace mineral stock s
provenance.base_medium: Shewanella oneidensis MR-1 defined medium (a.k.a. 'MR-1 medium'), lactate as electron donor, PIPES-buffered; modified here to 20 mM sodium lactate
provenance.base_medium_reference: Cited in paper as 'modified MR-1 medium (Cao et al., 2011)'. Two candidate Cao et al. 2011 papers on Shewanella sp. HRCR-1 biofilms from the Beyenal/Fredrickson group: (1) Cao B, et al. 2011, Environ Microbiol 13:1018-1031, DOI 10.1111/j.1462-2920.2010.02407.x, PMID 21251176; (2) Cao B, et al. 2011, Environ Sci Technol 45:5483-5490, DOI 10.1021/es200095j. Both grew HRCR-1/MR-1 in the standard S. oneidensis defined medium whose full composition is documented in the same lineage (e.g. Bretschger et al. 2007, Appl Environ Microbiol, DOI 10.1128/AEM.01087-07). Neither Cao 2011 full text nor the PMC6255981 methods reprint the recipe; composition below is the canonical S. oneidensis MR-1 defined medium repeatedly reported for this organism/group.
